similar_questions.title: Swapping Nodes in a Linked List
Reverse Nodes in Even Length Groups

discussion_posts.name: calm27
discussion_posts.content:
did in one pass, but with a small issue, i've spent ~4 hours to resolve it. 
interview -> failed
self-confidence -> up

discussion_posts.name: isaackleinman
discussion_posts.content: The description states that "k is a positive integer and is less than or equal to the length of the linked list.", yet one of test cases has list `[1]` and `k = 2`.

discussion_posts.name: GS2306
discussion_posts.content:
Test Case
[1,2], k = 3
is present in the test suite, which is wrong.

It is clearly mentioned in the question that k will be smaller than the length of the linked list

discussion_posts.name: spookie886
discussion_posts.content: this question is difficult i dont know when it will ever become easy for me

discussion_posts.name: Harish010818
discussion_posts.content:
First hard problem solved by myself — under an hour, with an optimal solution!  
Felt like magic after struggling for so long. From now on, I won’t feel bad about being from a non-tech background.

Wish me the best guys 😊 — your support means a lot!

discussion_posts.name: Bane19
discussion_posts.content: Encountered this in an interview today. Explained the full approach but got stuck in coding. Was only able solve to a point to reverse the list fully. Spent 2 hrs post interview to solve it. Don\'t look into the solutions. Solve on your own.
